title: Noncollinear spin textures and 90° domain walls in twisted XY magnets
authors: Guanghui Cheng, Shiva T. Konakanchi, Andres E. Llacsahuanga Allcca, Sanjeev Khare, Nithin Abraham, Yuqing Cao, Hechang Lei, Kenji Watanabe, Takashi Taniguchi, Pramey Upadhyaya, Yong P. Chen
abstract: Twisted moiré magnets are promising in exploring noncollinear magnetic phases, yet current experimental studies have been restricted to uniaxial magnets, limiting the accessible phase space. Here, we demonstrate noncollinear moiré magnetism based on XY magnet CrCl3. The tunneling magnetoconductance of twisted CrCl3 exhibits multiple field-driven transitions in small-twist-angle devices, attributed to the coexisting antiferromagnetic and ferromagnetic domains with distinct susceptibilities. The inferred spin configuration depends on the layer number, reflecting the interlayer coupling strength between twisted layers. This moiré magnetism is remarkably robust, persisting up to twisted double nine-layer stacks. Combined with micromagnetic simulations, we identify the ground state as the predicted "twisted-s" phase featuring 90° domain walls. Finally, we demonstrate voltage control of these noncollinear phases, highlighting the electrically tunable twist-spintronics.
summary: This work reports on the discovery and characterization of moiré magnetism in twisted $	ext{CrCl}_3$ XY magnets. By measuring tunneling magnetoconductance, the authors observe field-induced transitions indicative of coexisting antiferromagnetic and ferromagnetic domains. Crucially, they demonstrate that the noncollinear ground state, featuring $90^\circ$ domain walls, can be electrically tuned using bias voltage, advancing the field of twist-spintronics.
main_problem: The study aims to explore noncollinear magnetic phases in twisted magnets, overcoming the limitations imposed by previous studies restricted to uniaxial magnetic systems.
main_result: The authors experimentally demonstrate moiré magnetism in twisted $	ext{CrCl}_3$, identifying a robust ground state characterized by $90^\circ$ domain walls and showing voltage control over these noncollinear spin textures.
method: The research combines experimental measurements of tunneling magnetoconductance ($G(H)$) in magnetic tunnel junctions with micromagnetic simulations to model the spin configurations.
model_or_system: The physical system is twisted $	ext{CrCl}_3$, an easy-plane XY magnet, studied in natural few-layer, twisted double bilayer (tDB), and twisted double trilayer (tDT) configurations. The magnetic behavior is governed by interlayer coupling modulated by the twist angle.
key_observables: Tunneling magnetoconductance $G(H)$ and its derivative $|dG/dH|$, which exhibit field-driven kinks corresponding to magnetic phase transitions.
important_parameters: Twist angle (e.g., $0.5^\circ$, $1^\circ$), external magnetic field ($H$), bias voltage ($V_{	ext{bias}}$), and temperature ($T$).
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes that the tunneling probability is determined by the interlayer spin alignment, and the micromagnetic model captures the essential physics of the interlayer exchange coupling.
figures_summary: Figures illustrate the XY spin model and Moiré superlattice structure; $G(H)$ vs. $H$ curves show field-induced kinks for tDB and tDT; phase diagrams map out the dependence of magnetic phases on temperature and field, and bias control is shown via $G(H)$ vs. $V_{	ext{bias}}$.
paper_structure: The paper progresses by first establishing the experimental platform using $	ext{CrCl}_3$ in twisted bilayers (tDB), demonstrating the two-kink feature. It then extends the analysis to twisted double trilayers (tDT), revealing the noncollinear ground state and mapping out control mechanisms via temperature and bias voltage.
why_interesting: The ability to electrically tune magnetic phases via voltage control in a moiré magnet system provides a direct platform for studying spin-charge coupling and novel spintronic functionalities, which is relevant to open quantum systems dynamics.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

(B) Moiré superlattice of twisted CrCl3 with a twist angle 𝜃.
Figure 1D shows a representative junction of twisted CrCl3 with a twist angle of 𝜃 = 0.5°.
